summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the SRP implementation uses SHA-1 for password hashing at core/srp.js:44-45, demonstrating the use of a cryptographically broken hash function in a security-critical authentication protocol.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that SHA-1 is used in the SRP password verification flow, showing the use of a deprecated and broken cryptographic hash function.
actual_observed_result: The verification script successfully identified SHA-1 usage in the SRP implementation at the specified code locations, confirming that the makeX function uses sjcl.hash.sha1.hash() for password derivation in the SRP protocol.
verification_method: The verification script reads and analyzes the core/srp.js source code to detect SHA-1 usage in the authentication implementation. The proof signal is the presence of sjcl.hash.sha1.hash calls in the makeX function for password processing.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code inspection at core/srp.js:44-45 confirms SHA-1 is used for password hashing
The makeX function calls sjcl.hash.sha1.hash() twice for SRP x derivation
SHA-1 is cryptographically broken and deprecated by NIST
This compromises the security of SRP authentication

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the existing target repository checkout at /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_67122281/repo and mounts it into the container.
